body_txt: A situation that takes place when a character goes too far in their methods to achieve its goals, and the general feeling once all it's said and done is the sense that all the effort, despite the outcome , was almost for nothing. In this case, "Was it really worth it?" it's a valid question. Maybe killing their opponent made them just like their foes . Maybe they had to shatter their closest friends's best weapon (or worse, their actual best friend! ) In any case, the deeds raise serious moral questions for them, and the answer isn't necessarily "Hell yeah, it was Worth It !" Note that the question doesn't necessarily even have to be asked, nor must it be after the deed's been done. Not to be confused with What the Hell, Hero? , which is about the character being called out for purposefully doing flat-out evil acts; in this trope the other characters don't hold it against the sacrificer. They may justify their actions saying " I Did What I Had to Do ." If they decide it's not worth it after all, but only after the fact, it's My God, What Have I Done? . If the 'it' was something like an Heroic Sacrifice , this usually causes a Bittersweet Ending . After all, the Big Bad may be dead, but so is The Hero , and that's no cause for celebration. A subtrope of Armor-Piercing Question and Pyrrhic Victory . If a character's actions or victory eventually result in him being bored because of it, then you have a case of Victory Is Boring . Compare And Then What? . noreallife
